✅ What's Working

  • 95.8% answer rate — industry-leading call response
  • LSA calls grew 440% from Jan (50) to Mar (270)
  • GBP #1 rankings sustained across key Gilbert keywords in Jan & Apr
  • Prescott Valley LSA dominates: 322 calls in 4 months
  • Flagstaff GBP impressions growing (132→160 in Q1)
  • Multi-location Google Business Profile coverage established

⚠️ Areas to Watch

  • Gilbert keyword rankings dropped Feb–Mar (1→14-20 range), recovered Apr
  • GBP website clicks declining YoY for Gilbert (163→76 clicks Jan)
  • Flagstaff LSA still minimal (3 calls) — opportunity untapped
  • Tempe LSA tracking not yet fully differentiated from Gilbert
  • April data partial (through Apr 20) — full month metrics pending
  • Water softener/treatment keywords dropped from tracking in 2026

🎯 Recommendations

  • Investigate Feb–Mar Gilbert ranking drop — possible GBP suspension or algo change
  • Scale Flagstaff LSA budget — GBP shows strong impressions there
  • Create dedicated Tempe LSA tracking number for clean attribution
  • Water treatment keywords need fresh GBP content/posts
  • Leverage Prescott Valley LSA success model across other markets
  • Add review velocity campaign for Mesa and Cottonwood locations

🏆 Key Takeaways — Month-Over-Month

  • February Surge: All channels spiked in Feb — LSA campaigns fully launched, GBP traffic recovered, Google Ads scaled up. Feb total calls (+188% vs Jan).
  • March Peak Performance: 906 total calls — highest month. LSA hit all-time high of 270 calls. PPC calls doubled. Organic search hit best-ever 36 calls.
  • April on Track: Partial April data (through Apr 20) shows 395 calls — projecting ~580-620 for full month, which would represent continued strength.
  • GBP Feb Spike Explanation: Feb GBP calls jumped to 375 (vs 230 Jan) — likely driven by increased local search demand and stronger map pack rankings after campaign ramp-up.
  • LSA Best ROI Signal: Prescott Valley LSA has delivered the strongest consistent growth (0 → 97 → 154 calls in 3 months). Budget there is generating exceptional returns.

LSA vs GBP Spam
4% vs 25%
LSA has 6× less spam noise

💡 Why LSA Has Far Less Spam Than GBP

LSA calls are routed through Google's verified Local Services Ads system — callers must go through Google's booking interface before reaching your tracking number. This acts as a natural filter. GBP phone numbers are publicly visible in Google Maps search results and easily scraped by robocall operations. Recommendation: Where possible, direct more call volume through LSA to improve signal quality and reduce wasted dispatch time on spam calls.

🏷️ Status Legend

🟢 Booked Appointment scheduled with time window confirmed 🟢 Valid Lead Answered with clear service intent, booking likely 🔵 Existing – Booked Return client, new appointment made 🔵 Existing Client Return client call, no new booking this call 🟡 Callback Gathering quotes or said they'd call back 🟡 Inquiry Asked questions, no appointment made 🔴 Spam Robocall, automated system, or spam 🟣 Marketing Solicitation / vendor calling Gold Star ⚫ Disconnected Dropped or hung up early 📩 Voicemail Left a voicemail 📵 Missed Not answered

📊 All Locations — Monthly GBP Profile Views Comparison

✅ GBP Strengths

  • Gilbert: Highest call volume (162 calls) and clicks (313) — flagship location
  • Flagstaff: 97.7% answer rate on tracked calls — operationally excellent
  • Tempe: GBP views rising (794 views in Jan) — building momentum
  • Prescott Valley: March call spike (37 calls) aligns with LSA growth

⚠️ Concerns

  • Gilbert Feb GBP calls dropped to 27 (from 46 in Jan) — possible algorithm issue
  • All locations show declining clicks vs. 2024 baseline for Gilbert
  • Flagstaff impressions dipped in Feb (55 vs 132 Jan) — needs investigation
  • April is partial — numbers will improve through month-end

🎯 Action Items

  • Add weekly GBP posts for all 4 locations to boost impressions
  • Run review request campaign for Tempe and Flagstaff profiles
  • Add service area and attributes to Prescott Valley listing
  • Investigate Gilbert GBP — check for duplicate listings
Source: Data from goldstarplumbingaz.com GBP REPORT — Keyword Ranking tab. Rankings represent Google Maps pack position. Green = positions 1–3 · Yellow = 4–10 · Red = 11+ · — = Not ranking / no data

⚠️ Spam Call Analysis — Why Are There So Many Spam Calls on GBP?

Gold Star's GBP tracking number received 179 confirmed spam/solicitation calls between Jan–Apr 2026, with a dramatic spike of 118 spam calls in February alone (vs. 8 in January). Here's what happened and what to do:

🔍 What Caused the Feb Spike (118 calls)

  • Coordinated 928-area-code robocall campaign: 99 of the 118 February spam calls originated from +1 (928) xxx-xxxx numbers — the Lake Havasu City / Kingman, AZ area code. This is a known robocall operation that scrapes Google Maps for local business phone numbers and calls them with fake "your listing may be suspended" recordings.
  • Impersonation of Google Support: Several calls played a message claiming the business's GBP listing had issues requiring immediate action. These are phishing attempts designed to trick businesses into surrendering GBP login credentials.
  • High GBP visibility = higher spam target rate: Gold Star's #1 map pack rankings across 4 markets makes the phone number highly visible in Google Maps — which also means it's easily scraped by spam operations.
  • Automated dialing with hang-ups: Average duration was only 26 seconds. 77 of the 118 calls lasted under 30 seconds — consistent with auto-dialers that drop when a human answers.

📋 Spam Breakdown by Month

MonthSpamMarketingTotal Noise% of Month
January 20266101615%
February 20261092113035%
March 202640145420%
April 2026 (1–20)1372016%
Total1685222025% overall

February spike tapered by March — the campaign appears to have paused or shifted, though a residual ~40 spam calls/month remains ongoing.

✅ What To Do — Recommended Actions

  • 1. Enable CallRail Spam Blocker: CallRail's spam protection feature automatically screens calls with known robocall signatures. This alone would have blocked the 928-prefix campaign.
  • 2. Block 928-area-code repeat offenders: Pull the 99 specific +1928 numbers from February and add them to CallRail's block list. Go to CallRail → Account Settings → Call Blocking.
  • 3. Report to Google: Use Google's "Report a problem" in Google Maps to report the GBP listing phone number as being targeted by spam. Google can apply additional call filtering at the listing level.
  • 4. Report to FTC: File a complaint at donotcall.gov for the robocall campaign. The 928-area-code operation is likely hitting hundreds of businesses — an FTC report helps trigger investigations.
  • 5. Never respond to "your listing is at risk" calls: Google never calls businesses via automated systems about listing suspension. All real GBP notifications come through the Google Business Profile dashboard only.
  • 6. Monitor monthly: Add a "Spam %" metric to this report each month. If it climbs above 20%, investigate immediately — it may indicate a new campaign or competitor-driven spam attack.

🏷️ Status Legend

🟢 Booked Appointment scheduled with time window confirmed 🟢 Valid Lead Answered with clear service intent, booking likely 🔵 Existing – Booked Return client, new appointment made 🔵 Existing Client Return client call, no new booking this call 🟡 Callback Gathering quotes or said they'd call back 🟡 Inquiry Asked questions, no appointment made 🔴 Spam Robocall, automated system, or spam 🟣 Marketing Solicitation / vendor calling Gold Star ⚫ Disconnected Dropped or hung up early 📩 Voicemail Left a voicemail 📵 Missed Not answered

📈 Calls by Source — Monthly Trend

🥧 Call Volume by Source (Total)

🔍 Key Observations & Hot Prospects

  • GBP dominates at 37%: 873 Google Business Profile calls — the strongest single channel. Maintaining GBP health and reviews is mission-critical.
  • LSA is the fastest-growing channel: 50 calls in January → 270 in March (440% growth). Prescott Valley alone generated 154 calls in March — best single-location month.
  • PPC is scaling rapidly: Landing page + Google Ads calls jumped from 19 (Jan) to 153 (Mar) — a 1,526% ramp. PMAX campaign is likely contributing to the volume growth.
  • Top hot prospect — WATSON DELBERTA: 20-minute LSA call from Oklahoma City suggests a referral or property owner with major plumbing work. Follow-up recommended.
  • Flagstaff is underperforming LSA: Only 3 LSA calls despite strong GBP impressions and 54 website calls. Budget allocation to Flagstaff LSA could unlock significant volume.
  • Return callers = 687 (29%): Nearly 1-in-3 callers are return clients — strong indicator of customer loyalty and repeat business. Excellent for lifetime value optimization.

✅ CallRail Strengths

  • 95.8% answer rate — exceptional operational performance across all locations
  • 2,358 total calls in just 4 months — high lead volume across channels
  • LSA calls scaled 440% Jan→Mar — best ROI channel by growth rate
  • GBP remains #1 source at 37% — strong organic local presence
  • 70.9% new callers — healthy pipeline of fresh prospects
  • Multi-location tracking provides granular attribution data

⚠️ Issues & Gaps

  • 98 missed calls (4.2%) — at this volume that's ~24 missed jobs/month
  • Flagstaff LSA only 3 calls — major untapped market with proven GBP demand
  • LSA-PV2 secondary tracker: 73 calls in Feb → only 7 in Mar — unexplained drop; investigate campaign status
  • No Meta/Facebook dedicated tracking number — social ROI unmeasurable
  • Yelp declined from 32 (Feb) to 15 (Apr) — worth monitoring
  • April already 15 missed calls in partial month — may need additional staffing

🎯 Recommendations

  • Add dedicated CallRail tracking number for Meta Ads/Facebook campaigns
  • Activate Flagstaff LSA budget — demand is proven via GBP and website calls (54+)
  • Investigate LSA-PV2 secondary tracker Feb→Mar drop; restore if campaign paused
  • Review missed calls daily — consider after-hours answering service for 4.2% capture
  • Import CallRail conversions directly into Google Ads for automated bid optimization
  • Tag and score return callers to identify high-value recurring customers for outreach

PMAX — Prescott Valley (Performance Max)Running · Spend data not in keyword report
Performance Max — Active (spend in separate PMAX report)

⚡ PMAX Campaign Note

  • Performance Max is active for the Prescott Valley market and operates across all Google inventory: Search, Display, YouTube, Maps, Gmail, and Discover.
  • PMAX campaigns do not expose traditional keyword-level data — performance is tracked at the campaign level through Google Ads interface only.
  • PMAX is likely contributing to the significant increase in landing page calls (19 Jan → 153 Mar). Combined with Search campaigns, this drives the PPC call volume shown in CallRail.
  • Recommend: Pull PMAX-specific conversion data from Google Ads interface monthly for this report.

best plumbing service$110.1541560—— 0 conversions

🔴 Zero-Conversion Keywords

Three keywords — "plumbers cottonwood arizona," "local plumbers," and "best plumbing service" — spent $463.98 combined with zero tracked conversions. Pause or add to negative list.

🔴 High CPL on Top Keyword

"plumber prescott" is top spend at $833.24 with a $185.16 CPL — nearly 1.5x the account average. Improve landing page relevance and QS to reduce this cost.

🟡 PMAX Visibility Gap

Performance Max campaign is live but spend and conversion data is not in keyword export. This creates a blind spot in CPL reporting. Pull PMAX data monthly.

🟡 Quality Score Opportunity

"plumber in prescott az" has QS 5 despite being the account's best CPL keyword ($69.42). Improving QS 5→7 could reduce CPC by 20–30%, dramatically improving efficiency.

🟢 Brand Keywords Outperform

Brand terms ("goldstar plumbing" QS 10, "gold star plumbing" QS 10) deliver the lowest CPL in the account: $53.71 and $36.25 respectively. Protect brand campaign budget.

🔵 PPC Call Volume Scaling

CallRail shows PPC calls grew from 19 (Jan) to 153 (Mar) — a 1,526% ramp. Combined Search + PMAX is generating significant lead flow. Attribution to PMAX likely significant.

🎯 Priority Recommendations — Google Ads

  • 1. Pause zero-conversion keywords: "plumbers cottonwood arizona," "local plumbers," and "best plumbing service" have spent $463.98 with no tracked conversions. Reallocate to top performers.
  • 2. Improve QS on "plumber in prescott az": This keyword has the best CPL ($69.42) but QS 5. Dedicated landing page with exact match content could push QS to 8+, reducing CPC significantly.
  • 3. Pull PMAX performance monthly: Add PMAX campaign metrics to this report dashboard — segmented by asset group and audience signal for full attribution clarity.
  • 4. Import CallRail conversions to Google Ads: Setting up offline conversion import from CallRail → Google Ads enables smart bidding to optimize toward actual calls, not just clicks.
  • 5. Expand to additional markets: Current campaigns are Prescott Valley-focused. With LSA success in Gilbert and Flagstaff GBP demand, consider Search campaigns for those markets.
  • 6. Negative keyword audit: "plumber s" keyword (broad match issue) and Cottonwood with 0 conversions suggest broad match waste. Tighten to phrase and exact match.

Campaign Overview — Jan 1 – Apr 20, 2026

CampaignStatusSpendImpressionsReachLeadsCPLEndsNotes
Lead Gen - Jan 2026 - LP Inactive $1,249.88 25,604 11,519 24 $52.08 Feb 3, 2026 Initial test campaign — higher CPL, ended after 34 days as daily budget campaign launched
Lead Gen - Daily Budget - LP ✓ Active $3,634.53 68,225 29,452 160 $22.72 Ongoing Optimized ongoing campaign — CPL dropped 56% vs. initial; 87% of all Meta leads
TOTAL — $4,884.41 93,829 40,971 184 $26.54 avg — Attribution: 7-day click, 1-day view or 1-day engaged view

📊 Meta Ads Performance Highlights — Jan–Apr 2026

💰 Spend Efficiency

  • Total spend: $4,884.41 over 110 days
  • Daily avg: $44.40/day
  • CPL improved 56% from $52 → $22 as campaign matured
  • Active campaign is now at optimal learning phase

📈 Lead Volume

  • 184 pixel leads total (form fills / landing page)
  • Active campaign: 160 leads at $22.72 CPL
  • Landing page: goldstarplumbingaz.com/trusted-plumber-arizona/
  • CallRail tracked 13 inbound calls from the same landing page

🎯 Reach & Awareness

  • 93,829 impressions · 40,971 unique reach
  • Frequency: ~2.3× per person
  • Active campaign 3× the reach of initial test
  • Attribution: 7-day click + 1-day view

🎯 Meta Ads Potential — Market Sizing

Homeowners in PV
~42K
Target Audience
35–65

Prescott Valley / Prescott metro has high homeownership rate — ideal Facebook audience for plumbing services

Suggested Content Calendar — Social Posts

#Suggested Post ThemePlatformGoalFormat
1Before/after water heater replacement — Prescott Valley jobFacebook + IGTrust / LeadPhoto carousel
2"Signs your water softener needs replacing" — educationalFacebookAwarenessShort video
35-star Google review spotlight — "Fast response at 2am"Facebook + IGSocial proofGraphic
4Emergency drain cleaning promotion — summer specialFacebookLeadsAd + offer
5Technician introduction video — "Meet the Gold Star team"Facebook + IGBrand trustShort video
6Flagstaff market launch announcement — now serving NAZFacebookAwarenessPhoto + caption

✅ Meta Strengths & Opportunities

  • Homeowner demographic (35–65) on Facebook perfectly matches Gold Star's customer profile
  • Before/after content performs extremely well for service trades on social
  • Emergency plumbing messaging creates high urgency — ideal for Facebook Leads Ads
  • Retargeting GBP/website visitors with Meta pixel can recapture warm leads
  • Instagram is strong for showcasing water damage, renovation, and before/after work
  • Local awareness campaigns at $500–$1,000/mo could generate 30–80 additional leads/month

⚠️ Current Issues

  • Zero Meta tracking numbers in CallRail — no attribution visibility
  • Only 3 social-attributed calls in 4 months — major underperformance vs potential
  • Meta Ads Manager connected — 2 campaigns tracked Jan–Apr 2026
  • No Meta pixel installed on goldstarplumbingaz.com — no retargeting audiences built
  • No Facebook Lead Ads running — leaving the lowest-friction lead capture unused
  • February Pittman call (6m 52s) shows organic social demand exists but uncaptured

🎯 Priority Recommendations

  • Install Meta Pixel on goldstarplumbingaz.com to start building retargeting audiences immediately
  • Add CallRail tracking numbers to Facebook/Instagram ad landing pages for proper ROI measurement
  • Launch Facebook Lead Ads for emergency plumbing, drain cleaning, and water heater replacement in Prescott Valley + Gilbert markets
  • Scale the active campaign — CPL has dropped from $52 to $22 as the algorithm optimized; increasing daily budget now captures leads at proven cost
  • Retarget website visitors who didn't call — serve them urgency-focused ads within 7 days
  • Set test budget at $750/mo across 3 markets to establish CPL baseline before scaling

📊 Call Volume Analysis — What's Driving Increases & Decreases

Month-over-month breakdown · Root cause analysis · Recommendations by source

January
81
Baseline month
February
129
↑ +59% vs Jan
March
194
↑ +50% vs Feb · Peak
April (1–20)
85
Partial · ~128 projected
Booking Rate
69%
Jan 74% → Mar 70% → Apr 61%
Peak Day
Mon
111 calls · Wed 2nd (103)

📈 Jan → Feb: +59% (+48 calls) — What Caused the Surge

  • Yelp activated (0 → 32 calls, +32): The single biggest driver. Yelp generated zero calls in January then suddenly delivered 32 in February — strongly suggesting a new Yelp Business listing was set up or ad spend began around late January. This one source accounts for 67% of the Feb increase.
  • Direct calls doubled (12 → 27, +15): Growing brand awareness — more customers are calling directly without searching. This compounds over time as reputation builds across all markets.
  • Google Organic grew (17 → 22, +5): Gradual SEO improvement, consistent with multi-month organic ranking gains taking effect post-Q4 2025 campaign work.
  • Website-Prescott stable (25 → 27): Core Prescott market performing consistently — existing SEO holding position.

📊 Feb Source Breakdown

SourceJanFebChange
Google Organic1722↑ +29%
Yelp032↑ New
Website-Prescott2527↑ +8%
Website-Flagstaff77→ Flat
Website-PV04↑ New
Direct1227↑ +125%
Directory / Maps1013↑ +30%

🚀 Feb → Mar: +50% (+65 calls) — What Drove the Peak

  • Website-PV exploded (4 → 34, +750%): Prescott Valley organic landing pages hit critical ranking mass in March. This is the clearest SEO win — new service area content or backlinks pushed PV pages onto page 1, driving a 8.5× call increase. This suggests a deliberate content or technical SEO push around Feb–Mar.
  • Website-Flagstaff surged (7 → 26, +271%): Flagstaff market SEO results arrived in March. Likely driven by service area page optimization and GBP ranking improvements in the Flagstaff market that were being built through Jan–Feb.
  • Google Organic peaked (22 → 36, +64%): Spring plumbing season plus improving organic rankings — water heater, drain, and sewer keywords gaining page 1 visibility. March is historically the start of busy season for plumbing.
  • Direct hit peak (27 → 36, +33%): Accumulated brand recognition — customers who saw the business across multiple channels (GBP, Yelp, ads) now calling directly. This is a compounding effect of all-channel activity.
  • All sources firing simultaneously: March was the first month all 9 sources contributed meaningful call volume — the compound effect of every channel active at once.

📊 Mar Source Breakdown

SourceFebMarChange
Google Organic2236↑ +64%
Yelp3224↓ -25%
Website-Prescott2722↓ -19%
Website-Flagstaff726↑ +271%
Website-PV434↑ +750%
Direct2736↑ +33%
Directory / Maps1312→ Stable

⚠️ Concerns — Sources Declining or Flat

  • Yelp declining (32 → 24 → 6 in Apr partial): After a strong Feb, Yelp calls have dropped sharply every month. This typically means: (1) Yelp ads were paused or budget reduced, (2) review count/rating dropped affecting Yelp search rank, or (3) the listing was flagged. Action needed: Check Yelp Business dashboard for impression/click trends.
  • Website-Prescott declining (25 → 27 → 22 → 3 in Apr): Prescott was the top organic source in Jan but dropped to near-zero in April. This is the sharpest single decline and likely indicates a ranking drop for core Prescott keywords — possibly a competitor gaining ground or a technical issue on the Prescott service area page.
  • Booking rate slipping (74% Jan → 61% Apr): More calls are coming in but fewer are converting to bookings. April shows 18 disconnects and 9 not-booked — worth reviewing call recordings to identify if dispatch response time or script is creating friction.
  • Apple Maps stalled (5 in Mar → 0 in Apr): Apple Maps volume dried up completely in April. This could be a listing issue — verify the Apple Maps / Apple Business Connect listing is active and has correct info.

📅 Call Volume by Day of Week

Understanding when organic callers reach out helps align staffing and callback priority.

DayCalls% of Total
Monday11123%
Wednesday10321%
Thursday8718%
Tuesday7916%
Friday6714%
Saturday286%
Sunday143%

Monday + Wednesday = 44% of all organic calls. Weekend coverage minimal — missed calls on Sat/Sun could represent lost booked jobs.

✅ Recommendations — What To Do Now

🚀 Double Down on What's Working

  • Scale Website-PV content: Prescott Valley went from 0 → 34 calls in 3 months. Create more service-specific landing pages for PV (water heater, drain cleaning, sewer) to compound this growth further.
  • Invest in Website-Flagstaff: Flagstaff hit 26 calls in March after only 7 in Jan/Feb — the market is responding. Add Flagstaff-specific service pages and build local citations.
  • Protect Google Organic rankings: March peak of 36 calls represents the best Google Organic month. Publish monthly blog content to defend keyword positions going into the busy summer season.
  • Leverage Monday & Wednesday: 44% of all calls arrive Mon + Wed. Ensure full dispatch staffing those two days. Consider running Google Ads bid boosts on Mon/Wed mornings.

🔧 Fix What's Declining

  • Audit Yelp listing immediately: 32 → 6 calls is a 81% drop in 2 months. Log into Yelp Business and check: (1) impression trend, (2) current star rating, (3) whether Yelp Ads are still active. A single removed review or paused ad can explain this entirely.
  • Investigate Website-Prescott ranking drop: 22 calls in March → 3 in April is a 86% collapse. Run a Google Search Console query for Prescott-related keywords and check if the service area page lost ranking. Likely caused by a competitor gaining position or a thin-content penalty.
  • Recover April booking rate: Booking rate dropped from 70% (Mar) to 61% (Apr). Pull the 9 "Not Booked" April call recordings and identify if there's a common objection (price, availability, area coverage) the dispatch team can address with a script change.
  • Reactivate Apple Maps: Zero Apple Maps calls in April. Claim/verify the Apple Business Connect listing and ensure the phone number and service area are correct — this is a free call source that should be generating 3–5 calls/month minimum.

📅 30-Day Action Checklist

☐ Check Yelp Business dashboard for impression/ad status ☐ Run GSC audit on Prescott service area page rankings ☐ Verify Apple Business Connect listing is active ☐ Review Apr "Not Booked" call recordings (9 calls) ☐ Publish 2 new Prescott Valley service pages ☐ Add Flagstaff drain cleaning + water heater pages ☐ Ensure full dispatch staffing Mon & Wed mornings ☐ Publish 1 blog post targeting "plumber near me" intent

Local Rankings Snapshot — Year-Over-Year (Jan 2025 vs Apr 2026)

MarketKeywordJan 2025 RankJan 2026 RankApr 2026 RankYoY ChangeQoQ Change
GilbertPlumber1141Recovered↑ +13 pos
GilbertDrain cleaning1201Recovered↑ +19 pos
GilbertWater heater repair1—12↓ DroppedPartial recovery

Prescott ValleyDrain cleaning111Stable #1Unchanged

FlagstaffSewer line repair—11Strong entryStable
TempePlumber—21New market↑ Hit #1
TempeWater heater repair—11Strong entryStable

🔴 Gilbert Feb–Mar Rankings Collapse

Gilbert keyword rankings dropped from #1 to #14–20 in Jan–Feb 2026. Root cause: likely Google algorithm update or GBP spam filter trigger. Rankings recovered to #1 by April — suggests temporary issue, not permanent damage.

🔴 Water Softener Keyword Gap

Water softener installation ranks only in Flagstaff (#2). All other markets show no ranking. This is a high-value service category with significant revenue potential — dedicated GBP content and service pages needed.

🟡 Low CTR on High-Volume Keywords

"Plumber near me" generates 8,848 impressions at avg position 8.0 but only 0.08% CTR (7 clicks). "Plumber gilbert az" gets 2,758 impressions at position 14.8. Moving these to top 3 could add 200–400+ organic clicks/month.

🟡 Traffic Dominated by Paid — Organic Underperforming

Paid Other (programmatic/display) drives 3,971 sessions (31%) vs Google Organic 2,372 (18%). Organic search engagement rate is 62.7% vs paid's 19.1% — organic visitors convert 3× better but get less budget focus.

🟢 Map Pack Dominance — 3+ Markets

Prescott Valley, Flagstaff, and Tempe all rank #1 for core plumbing keywords. This map pack dominance is the primary driver of GBP's 873 calls — the single largest traffic source at 37%.

🔵 New Markets Ranking Fast

Flagstaff and Tempe launched as new GBP locations in 2024–2025. Both have achieved #1 rankings across core terms within months — evidence of strong foundational SEO setup and effective citation building.

✅ SEO Strengths

  • Dominant map pack presence — #1 rankings across Flagstaff, PV, Tempe for all core terms
  • 852,907 GSC impressions prove strong brand search visibility and growing local footprint
  • Website sessions grew 77% from Jan (2,286) to Mar (4,057) — accelerating organic growth
  • Organic search engagement rate 62.7% — far above site average of 33.2%, confirming high organic visitor intent
  • Blog content proven to drive impressions — anode rod article: 59,817 impressions at position 2.0
  • New markets (Tempe, Flagstaff) achieving top rankings within their first year — strong SEO foundation
  • Gilbert rankings fully recovered to #1 after Feb–Mar algorithm-related dip

⚠️ SEO Issues

  • Overall site avg position 23.6 — majority of rankings on page 2–3; non-branded local terms underperforming
  • Gilbert water heater repair ranking at #12 — high-revenue keyword underperforming
  • Water softener/treatment keywords absent in Gilbert, Tempe, Prescott Valley
  • GBP impressions declining in Gilbert (from 1,920 in Dec 2024 → 451 in Feb 2026 → partial recovery)
  • Site engagement rate at 33.2% — high bounce rate (66.8%) signals landing page relevance or speed issues
  • Overall CTR of 0.21% is below the 1–3% benchmark for local service businesses — title/meta description optimization needed
  • "Plumber near me" at 8,848 impressions, pos 8 — getting just 7 clicks; top 3 position would yield ~300+ clicks/month

🎯 SEO Priorities

  • Optimize meta titles/descriptions for top impression pages — homepage (22K impr) and Flagstaff (39K impr) have low CTR; improving to 1% would add 400–600 clicks/month
  • Recover Gilbert water heater ranking — build citations, add service-specific GBP posts, request reviews mentioning water heater service
  • Create water softener service pages for Gilbert, Tempe, PV — high-value missed category
  • Accelerate blog content — anode rod blog (59,817 impressions) proves content drives visibility; target 2 posts/month on water heater, sewer, and Prescott service keywords
  • Improve Prescott organic page — 50,141 impressions at position 32.9 — a strong page optimization push could move this to page 1
  • GBP post cadence — 2x/week posts across all locations to sustain map pack dominance and drive GBP click-through
  • CRO audit — with 12,843 sessions and 33.2% engagement rate, fixing page speed and CTA placement could double call volume without increasing traffic

📝 Suggested Blog Topics — SEO Content Calendar

  • Week 1: "Why Is My Water Heater Making Noise? (Gilbert & Prescott Valley Homeowners Guide)" — target water heater repair keywords
  • Week 2: "Do I Need a Water Softener in Prescott Valley, AZ?" — target water softener installation search in hard water market
  • Week 3: "Trenchless Sewer Repair vs Traditional Digging — What Flagstaff Homeowners Need to Know"
  • Week 4: "How Much Does a Plumber Cost in Tempe, AZ? 2026 Pricing Guide"
  • Week 5: "Top 5 Signs Your Sewer Line Needs Repair (Prescott & Cottonwood)"
  • Week 6: "Best Tankless Water Heater Brands for Arizona Homes — Gold Star's Expert Picks"
